title: RHCSA 10 Mock Exam G
description: A RHCSA 10 mock exam focused on RHEL 10 administration, Flatpak, systemd timers, storage, networking, users, security, and services.

tasks: Set hostname to clientg.exam10.lab and map serverg.exam10.lab to 192.168.122.3.
Configure System eth1 with IPv4 address 192.168.122.66/24, gateway 192.168.122.1, and DNS 192.168.122.3.
Create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions using http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
Create system Flatpak remote examgflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo with GPG verification disabled.
Install org.rhcsa.Tools from examgflatpak, then remove it after verification.
Create group teamg10, create user userg10, set password cinder9, and add the user to teamg10.
Allow %teamg10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password by using a sudoers drop-in.
Set maximum password age for userg10 to 51 days and warning period to 7 days.
Create /usr/local/bin/g-who that prints the primary group for the supplied user argument.
Write users whose shell ends with sh to /root/g-shell-users.txt.
Create gzip archive /root/g-etc.tar.gz containing /etc/hosts and /etc/fstab.
Create /root/g-original, hard link /root/g-hard, and symlink /root/g-soft.
Create and enable examgtimer.timer that runs every 10 minutes.
Create VG vgg10 and LV datag mounted at /mnt/datag10.
Allow TCP port 8106 permanently in firewalld and reload.
Create /var/www/html/g.html and restore its default SELinux context.
Persistently enable httpd_can_network_connect.
Activate the throughput-performance tuned profile.
Configure persistent systemd journal storage.
Create a cron job for userg10 that writes EXAM10 to /home/userg10/exam10.log every 15 minutes.
Set the default target to multi-user.target without rebooting.
Install lsof and ensure tcpdump is removed.
hints: Make changes persistent unless a question says otherwise.
Use local repositories and local documentation; do not depend on internet access.
Flatpak and systemd timer tasks belong to the RHCSA 10 track.
checks: connection_name="System eth1"; nmcli -g NAME connection show "$connection_name" >/dev/null 2>&1 || connection_name="$(private_dev="$(ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$4 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $2; exit}')"; nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: -v private_dev="$private_dev" '$2 == private_dev {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$connection_name" && nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.66/24' && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.3' && nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx manual
getent group teamg10 >/dev/null && id -nG userg10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx teamg10
systemctl cat examgtimer.timer | grep -Fqx 'OnCalendar=*:0/10' && systemctl is-enabled examgtimer.timer | grep -qx enabled
solution_outline: Work through each question independently and verify before moving on.

solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.66/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF
